column5: System rounds down, rejects, or enforces integers only.

column5: Input is restricted to numeric values only.
column6: HTML type=number prevents non-numeric input. Server parseInt() returns NaN → defaults to 0.
column10: Browser enforces numeric-only input. Server-side parseInt fallback provides additional safety.

column5: System handles the large number correctly without crashing.
column6: PostgreSQL INTEGER type holds up to ~2.1 billion. 99999999 is well within bounds. No crash expected.
column10: No upper ceiling enforced in current app logic beyond DB integer range. Functionally acceptable.

column5: System blocks transaction; prevents digital stock from exceeding physical.
column6: Server: new_stock = current_stock + quantity_change. If new_stock < 0 → ROLLBACK → 400 Insufficient stock.

column5: System blocks execution; error returned: "Allocation Breach".

column6: Server: new_stock = 6 < allocated_stock 8 → Allocation Breach returned.
column10: Confirmed: Same guardrail as TC-64. Available = current_stock - allocated_stock = 2; dispatch 4 fails.

column10: CRITICAL BUG: app.js line 246 sends transaction_type: 'restock' but DB CHECK constraint only allows 'addition'. Fix: change 'restock' to 'addition' in app.js. FIXED IN CODE — verify after fix.

column3: Staff needs to remove items fast.

column10: Confirmed: BEGIN → SELECT FOR UPDATE → UPDATE → INSERT → COMMIT pattern in server.js PUT route.
